---
title: "Projects"
page-layout: full
title-block-banner: true
---

A mix of class projects, replications, and experiments — most live as posts on this site, with full code on GitHub. Two threads run through almost everything: **measuring marketing impact honestly** and **building data workflows that don't fall over.**

::: {.project-grid}

::: {.project-card}
### 🧪 A/B Testing a Call to Action
*Simulating classical frequentist statistics from scratch*

Walked through the Law of Large Numbers, sampling distributions, and hypothesis testing by simulating an A/B test on two CTAs. Built intuition for *why* the t-test works rather than just calling `scipy.stats`.

**Stack:** Python · NumPy · matplotlib · simulation

[Read the post →](posts/){.project-link}
:::

::: {.project-card}
### 📈 Did the Minimum Wage Kill Jobs?
*Replicating Card & Krueger (1994) — Difference-in-Differences*

Re-ran the canonical NJ–PA minimum wage study using diff-in-diff. A clean exercise in causal inference: parallel trends, control selection, and why the "obvious" answer (wages up → jobs down) didn't show up in the data.

**Stack:** R · DiD · ggplot2 · econometrics

[Read the post →](posts/){.project-link}
:::

::: {.project-card}
### 💻 Post With Code
*Reproducible analysis with executable code blocks*

A working example of analysis-as-code: Quarto chunks that execute on render so the report and the code can never drift apart. Small project, but the principle scales.

**Stack:** Quarto · R · reproducibility

[Read the post →](posts/){.project-link}
:::

::: {.project-card}
### 🌐 This Website
*Personal site, customized Quarto theme*

The site you're reading. Built with Quarto, customized SCSS theming, custom card grid components, and deployed via GitHub Pages. Source on GitHub.

**Stack:** Quarto · SCSS · GitHub Pages

[View on GitHub →](https://github.com/rsm-y7niu){.project-link}
:::

:::

---

## In progress

I'm currently working on a few more things — a marketing mix modeling case study and a small ETL pipeline project. They'll show up here once they're in shape to share.

If you want to talk about any of this, find me on [LinkedIn](about.qmd) or [email](mailto:y7niu@ucsd.edu).
